title: The Masque of the Red Death
author: Edgar Allan Poe
short_description: A prince seals himself and his court inside a castle to escape a plague — only for Death itself to arrive as a masked guest.
description: Prince Prospero's masquerade ball becomes a meditation on the futility of hiding from mortality — one of literature's most vivid allegories of death's democracy.
benefits: Read a legally cleared digital text.

learnings: Understand the major themes and structure of the work.
Engage with the text in a clean digital format.
about_author: Edgar Allan Poe (1809–1849) was an American writer, poet, and critic, master of Gothic horror and inventor of the detective story.

chapters.title: The Masque of the Red Death

rights_basis: Edgar Allan Poe died 1849; first published 1842. Public domain in India and the U.S.
